title: xeroderris stuhlmannii
intro: Xeroderris stuhlmannii is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. It is also known as the poison nut due to its toxic compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The medicinal properties of Xeroderris stuhlmannii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and seeds are used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, but caution is advised due to its toxicity, and it should only be used under the guidance of a medical professional.
